page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring custom AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processors. It is configured with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The memory configuration includes 16.0 GB of RAM, translating to 4.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled as a 237 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows strong memory and compression capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression at 36.53 GB/sec and single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 17.27 GB/sec, alongside a top-tier PassMark memory score of 3831.3. However, general CPU compute benchmarks present some tradeoffs; the PassMark CPU score is weak at 5627.95, and the CPU encryption test is also weak at 1186.72. Multi-core stress-ng performance at 8022.08 and Redis SET operations at 13.22 million operations/sec fall within the average range. LLM inference on a medium 7B model is limited, with a poor prompt processing speed of 11.29 tokens/sec, though text generation remains average at 9.31 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m9gd.xlarge offers a balanced resource density, combining dedicated ARMv9 cores with high-speed local NVMe storage. This profile is suitable for general-purpose workloads, web serving, database operations, and memory-bound tasks that benefit from high memory bandwidth and local scratch space, while avoiding heavy cryptographic or high-performance computing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by a 4-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 16.0 GB of RAM, a 237 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and a baseline network speed of 2 Gbps. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier performance in memory benchmarks and single-threaded compression tasks, such as gzip compression at 36.53 GB/sec. However, it exhibits weaker performance in PassMark CPU and encryption tests. With its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and integrated high-speed local storage, the m9gd.xlarge is well-suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, caching layers, and database operations that require local scratch space, while offering a cost-efficient entry point for ARM-based deployments.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 ARMv9 cores, 16.0 GB RAM, and a 237 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory and compression performance for database and web workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.xlarge features 4 ARMv9 cores, 16.0 GB RAM, and a 237 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for general-purpose and database workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Neoverse-V3 cores, high-speed local NVMe storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth.
bullet_points: Powered by 4 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 cores at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB RAM and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD instance store
Top-tier performance in single-threaded compression and memory benchmarks
Tradeoffs include weaker performance in CPU encryption and general PassMark CPU tests
Suitable for general-purpose applications, web serving, and database operations
